blocks.content: The second 34 came at 5:33. She started packing her bag — the book, a folded schedule, a pen she clicked three times before putting it away.
She stood up and the schedule fell out of her bag. We both reached for it at the same time.
My hand got there first but she was already coming down and her fingers landed on mine before either of us pulled back.
A half-second. Maybe less. Neither of us moved.
Then she took the schedule and I let it go and she said 'thanks' quiet, not loud, the kind of quiet that means something else was in the word with it.
Like it was a question that already knew its answer.
She got on the bus. The doors closed. The bus pulled away. I stood on the curb and counted to ten before I let myself feel it.
